channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 3 killed when tree hits vehicle in Michigan and damage reported in Tennessee amid widespread storms
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — Three family members died after a tree struck their vehicle in Michigan as powerful storms swept across the region, authorities said, and more potentially dangerous weather was forecast Monday across parts of the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but a new AP-NORC poll finds his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk-funded political group spends big and goes door to door in the Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wis. (AP) — Elon Musk and his political operatives have tried to shape the closely watched Wisconsin Supreme Court race in ways that are not immediately obvious but could be critical to Tuesday's outcome.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mexican national convicted in deadly semi-truck crash in Colorado detained by immigration authorities
item.description: A suspected illegal migrant convicted in a deadly crash in Colorado last year involving the semi-truck he was driving was detained on Sunday by federal immigration authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their disagreements often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities investigate damaging fire at New Mexico's GOP headquarters
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, New Mexico (AP) — Federal authorities are investigating a fire early Sunday that damaged the entryway to the headquarters of the Republican Party of New Mexico in Albuquerque.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: See a missing police officer post? It's likely part of a nationwide scam, authorities say
item.description: Have you seen a post on Facebook recently, asking you to be on the lookout for a missing police officer? Chances are, you're actually seeing a scam post, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, WGN 9

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk hands out $1 million payments after Wisconsin Supreme Court declines request to stop him
item.description: Elon Musk has given out $1 million checks to two Wisconsin voters, declaring them spokespeople for his political group.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KTLA 5

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hailey Van Lith scores 26 points to lead TCU to 1st Elite Eight with 71-62 win over Notre Dame
item.description: TCU is in unchartered territory thanks to Hailey Van Lith, reaching the Elite Eight for the first time.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: The family of a 74-year-old man who died during a trip to Hawaii say they found the skeletal remains of a human being in a treehouse on his Georgia property a few days after his death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, Fox 5

item.title: Six hurricanes forecasted to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: A look at who has been detained or deported in a US crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: Supporters of Palestinian causes with ties to American universities have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lights, Camera, Relocation: Utahns react as Sundance films its final scene in Utah
item.description: Park City businesses are reacting to Sundance's decision to move to another location, with residents confident that the city will continue to be a world-class destination for arts and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Renisha Mall, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
